surface.name: Zoom General App creation build flow
surface.selection_basis: The Zoom Developer Platform home presents Start building as its primary action and links it to the unified General App creation build flow. That flow creates the OAuth client, collects redirect URLs and scopes, and uses Local Test Add App Now to obtain a user authorization code. Zoom's OAuth 2.0 documentation continues that same General App path: exchange the authorization code for an access token, then call Zoom REST APIs with the bearer token. First success is the first authenticated Zoom API response, not app creation alone.

alternatives_considered.name: Server-to-Server OAuth app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Server-to-Server OAuth is a separate app type with account_credentials tokens and no user consent. The platform home Start building path creates a General App, so the selected route continues that General App OAuth code exchange into an API call.

alternatives_considered.name: Zoom Video SDK for web
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a product and runtime-specific route with its own credential and toolkit branches rather than the vendor's unified General App start.

alternatives_considered.name: Zoom Meeting SDK for web
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a product and runtime-specific installation path rather than the vendor's unified General App start.

alternatives_considered.name: Zoom Apps in-client surface
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: In-client Zoom Apps are one feature path inside General App creation; the shared first-success boundary for API access remains OAuth token exchange plus an authenticated REST call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Zoom General App, complete OAuth authorization, obtain an access token, and receive a successful Zoom REST API response.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has chosen Zoom.
The developer has not yet created the General App being researched.
No Zoom account, developer role, product feature, or management type is silently assumed; documented branches cover those conditions.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Exchange the General App authorization code for an access token, then call GET /users/me on the Zoom REST API.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated Zoom REST API response returns the current user's profile JSON from GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/users/me.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Zoom API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body that includes the authenticated user's id and email or display identity fields.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Zoom OAuth 2.0 documents that Add App Now / Allow yields an authorization code, that POST https://zoom.us/oauth/token exchanges that code for an access_token, and that the access token authenticates Zoom API requests. The Users API documents GET /users/{userId} with userId=me as the authenticated current-user read.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: App creation and Local Test authorize the OAuth client but do not prove API access. Zoom's own OAuth guide treats token exchange and API use as the continuation of the General App authorization path. GET /users/me is the shortest authenticated product API call after that token is issued and does not require creating meetings or other product resources.

prerequisites.requirement: The account owner or an account admin role, or an assigned Zoom for developers role with View and Edit permissions.
Read Zoom Developer Platform Key concepts before creating the app.
A development OAuth redirect URL or endpoint and at least one OAuth allow-list URL.
A local Zoom client is needed to observe the documented My apps completion signal for a user-managed app.
Access to the email address used for signup is required if the developer must create a Zoom account, because Zoom sends a verification code.

primary_path.action: Open the Zoom Developer Platform home and select Start building.
details: The primary home-page action links to the Zoom App creation build flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The build-flow page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Review the build-flow overview and its What, Where, and Which questions.
details: The flow asks what kind of app is being created, where users will use it, and which Zoom products it will work with.
Use the Development view for the local-test path.
primary_path.input: Intended app and integration use case
primary_path.output: Inputs needed for the unified build flow
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can proceed to the Quick start guide.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation does not choose these use-case answers for the developer.

primary_path.action: Read Key concepts as required by the build-flow prerequisites.
details: Review app distribution labels, adding versus installing apps, and admin-managed versus user-managed apps.
primary_path.success_signal: The stated knowledge prerequisite is completed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Go to the Zoom App Marketplace and sign in with a Zoom account.
details: If no account exists, follow the documented account-creation branch before returning to sign in.
primary_path.success_signal: The Marketplace loads for the signed-in account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account creation requires email verification when the developer does not already have an account.

primary_path.action: Select Developer in the lower-left Marketplace navigation.
details: Zoom displays the Developer link only when the account has proper developer permissions.
primary_path.success_signal: The Developer link is visible and opens Created apps.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the link is absent, an account admin must assign the required role permissions.

primary_path.action: On Created apps, select Develop and then Build an app.
primary_path.success_signal: The app creation screen is shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Select General app and then Create.
primary_path.output: New General App in the build flow
primary_path.success_signal: The Basic Info page for the new app is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Generate a generic app name and development app credentials.
details: The build flow automatically generates a client ID and client secret.
Development credentials differ from production credentials.
primary_path.output: Generic name, development client ID, and development client secret
primary_path.success_signal: The generated values appear on Basic Info.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: Optionally replace the generated app name and save by clicking outside the name field.
details: Select the edit icon to change the name.
primary_path.success_signal: The new name is displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The generated name remains if the developer does not change it.

primary_path.action: Confirm whether the app is admin-managed or user-managed.
details: The management choice changes who can add the app and which products, features, scopes, and permissions are available.
Changing management type later requires reviewing and reconfirming features and scopes.
primary_path.input: Intended app manager and data-access level
primary_path.success_signal: Basic Info displays the chosen management type.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation provides no universal default because the choice depends on the app's intended users and access.

primary_path.action: Enter the required development OAuth Redirect URL or endpoint.
details: After a user approves the app, Zoom redirects an authorization code or token to this registered URL.
Loopback and development endpoints can be used in development but should not be used for production redirects.
primary_path.success_signal: The redirect URL is present in OAuth Information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unregistered or invalid redirect prevents the intended OAuth return path.

primary_path.action: Add the required OAuth allow-list URL or URLs.
details: Zoom accepts either a complete URL or a base URL without path or query parameters.
primary_path.input: Approved redirect domain or complete URL
primary_path.success_signal: The required allow-list entry is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Redirects outside the allow list are not accepted.

primary_path.action: Optionally enable Strict Mode URL or Subdomain check.
details: Strict mode permits only exact registered redirect matches.
Subdomain check restricts redirects to registered subdomains.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected restriction is reflected in OAuth Information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Exact or subdomain mismatches are rejected when the corresponding restriction is enabled.

primary_path.action: On Features, select and configure the Zoom product and feature path required by the intended General App.
details: Access supports tokens and event subscriptions.
Surface supports in-client Zoom products and features.
Embed provides Meeting, Contact Center, and Phone SDKs.
Connect configures third-party API connectors.
primary_path.input: The app's What, Where, and Which answers
primary_path.success_signal: The intended feature configuration is represented in the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Required fields vary by the feature path and are later checked by the Local Test wizard.

primary_path.action: On Scopes, select Add Scopes.
primary_path.success_signal: Zoom product and scope choices are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Available scopes depend on the app management type and selected features.

primary_path.action: Select the Zoom product, check the desired scopes, and select Done.
details: Scopes define which Zoom API methods the app is allowed to call.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected scopes appear on the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Scopes unnecessary for the app may be rejected by Zoom Security Review.

primary_path.action: Explain in Scope Description why each requested scope is necessary.
details: Zoom states that adding a scope submits a request to its Security Review team and that unnecessary scope requests may be rejected.
primary_path.success_signal: The scope justification is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation does not state a review duration for this first-mile path.

primary_path.action: Open Local Test in the Development view.
details: Local Test is available only in Development.
primary_path.success_signal: The Local Test wizard is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The page is not available in the Production view.

primary_path.action: Verify the app against all local-test requirements and display any incomplete fields.
primary_path.output: Requirement checklist or list of incomplete fields
primary_path.success_signal: No required field remains incomplete.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The developer must return to and complete any fields listed by the wizard before adding the app.

primary_path.action: Optionally select Preview Your App Listing Page.
details: The preview shows a summary of app details and the Marketplace listing page.
primary_path.success_signal: The preview is displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Preview is not required to take the documented Add App path.

primary_path.action: Select Add App Now for the developer's own account.
primary_path.input: App that passes Local Test requirements
primary_path.success_signal: The Allow action is presented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incomplete required fields block progression and are listed by the wizard.

primary_path.action: Review the authorization request and select Allow.
primary_path.input: Consent to add the development app
primary_path.success_signal: Zoom accepts the authorization and adds the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Declining authorization prevents the app from being added.

primary_path.action: Redirect to the configured OAuth redirect URI with an authorization code after Allow.
details: Zoom OAuth 2.0 states that after the user authorizes the app, Zoom redirects the browser to the redirect_uri and includes an authorization code such as https://example.com/callback?code=AUTH_CODE.
Add App Now on Local Test is the documented way to start that user authorization for the developer's own account.
primary_path.output: Authorization code on the redirect URI
primary_path.success_signal: The redirect URI receives a code query parameter.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Declining authorization prevents issuance of an authorization code.

primary_path.action: Capture the authorization code from the OAuth redirect request.
details: The application endpoint that owns the registered redirect URI reads the code query parameter.
primary_path.success_signal: The code value is available to the token exchange request.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or expired codes cannot be exchanged for tokens.

primary_path.action: Exchange the authorization code for an access token at Zoom's OAuth token endpoint.
details: Send POST https://zoom.us/oauth/token with grant_type=authorization_code, the authorization code, and the same redirect_uri used during authorization.
Authenticate the token request with HTTP Basic credentials using the development client ID and client secret.
primary_path.input: Authorization code, redirect_uri, development client ID, and client secret
primary_path.success_signal: Zoom returns JSON containing access_token, token_type bearer, expires_in, and scope.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid code, redirect_uri mismatch, or bad client credentials cause token exchange to fail.

primary_path.action: Call GET https://api.zoom.us/v2/users/me with the access token.
details: Include Authorization: Bearer <access_token>.
userId=me selects the authenticated user on the Users API Get a user operation.
The app must have requested a user read scope such as user:read:user during scope configuration.
primary_path.input: Bearer access token from the authorization_code exchange
primary_path.success_signal: Zoom returns HTTP 200 with JSON for the authenticated user.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing scopes, expired tokens, or invalid bearer credentials return API errors instead of the user profile.

primary_path.action: Confirm the Users API JSON identifies the authenticated Zoom user.
details: Inspect fields such as id and email or display name in the 200 response body.
primary_path.success_signal: The response body identifies the authorized Zoom user.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An empty or error body means API access has not succeeded.

branches.condition: The developer has no Zoom account.
branches.path: Open Zoom's signup page, enter a birth year, enter an email address, select Continue, wait for the verification email, enter the code, select Verify, enter full name and password, and select Continue. Zoom then creates a Basic account; return to Marketplace sign-in.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds identity data entry and an email-verification wait before the Marketplace path can begin.

branches.condition: The Developer link is absent because the account lacks app-development permissions.
branches.path: An administrator signs into the Zoom web portal, opens User Management, Roles, All, edits the relevant role, opens Role Settings, Advanced features, and enables both View and Edit for Zoom for developers. The developer then returns to the Marketplace.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces an administrator dependency before app creation can continue.

branches.path: Select Admin-managed. Account admins add and manage the app for users on their account. At the terminal local-test state the app is managed through the web portal and is hidden from the Zoom client.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Admin-managed apps change who can authorize the app and which scopes are available before the OAuth code exchange and API call.

branches.path: Select User-managed. Individual users add and manage the app, and each user must authenticate. At the terminal local-test state the app is displayed in My apps in the local Zoom client.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The final completion signal is directly visible in the Zoom client.

branches.condition: The app is an SDK app that does not need end-user authorization.
branches.path: Use the company's home domain in the required OAuth Redirect URL field, as directed by the OAuth Information page.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces an authorization callback endpoint with the company home domain for this SDK-specific condition.

branches.condition: The app needs API access or event notifications.
branches.path: Use Access to manage the app token and, if needed, configure Event Subscription with an event endpoint. Corresponding event scopes are selected automatically.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds credential handling or event endpoint configuration before Local Test authorization, token exchange, and the API call.

branches.condition: Users will open the app inside Zoom products.
branches.path: Use Surface to select the Zoom products, enter the Home URL domain, add accepted URLs to the Domain Allow List, select applicable in-client features, and choose supported Zoom clients. The Home URL must return the required OWASP response headers.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds hosted-application, domain, security-header, product, and client-support configuration before Local Test authorization, token exchange, and the API call.

branches.condition: The app embeds Zoom capabilities in a third-party application.
branches.path: Use Embed and select or download the applicable Meeting, Contact Center, or Phone SDK.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an SDK choice and SDK-specific work beyond the generic local-add milestone.

branches.condition: The app connects third-party APIs into Zoom.
branches.path: Use Connect, upload an OpenAPI 3.0.0 through 3.1.0 JSON or YAML file or enter the base URL and authentication parameters, then configure and test GET, POST, DELETE, PATCH, or PUT endpoints.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an API description, authentication, endpoint configuration, and endpoint test before Local Test authorization, token exchange, and the API call.

friction_gates.description: A Zoom account is required to sign in to the App Marketplace.
New account creation requires an email verification code after age and email entry.
The Developer link is exposed only to an owner, admin, or role with Zoom for developers permissions.
The developer must choose admin-managed or user-managed, which changes products, features, scopes, permissions, and the final verification surface.
A development OAuth redirect URL or endpoint is required.
At least one OAuth allow-list URL is required.
The developer must map the use case to applicable Zoom products and the Access, Surface, Embed, or Connect feature path.
Requested scopes determine allowed API methods and are subject to Zoom's stated minimal-access review principle.
The Local Test wizard blocks progression by listing incomplete required fields that must be resolved.
The developer must select Allow on Zoom's authorization screen to add the app.
Development client ID and client secret are required to exchange the authorization code for an access token.
A user read scope such as user:read:user must have been granted for GET /users/me to succeed.

uncertainties.question: Which minimum user-read scope string is currently required on a fresh General App for GET /users/me?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The live Marketplace scope picker labels and Users API scope list need a dated fresh-account confirmation for the current granular scope names.
uncertainties.impact: Scope labels have migrated toward granular scopes; an outdated scope selection can block the API call after a successful token exchange.

uncertainties.question: Which management type is universal for a General App?
uncertainties.impact: The final verification surface differs: My apps for user-managed apps versus web-portal management for admin-managed apps.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Zoom's official quickstart requires the developer to confirm the choice based on who will add and manage the app; it does not specify a universal default.

uncertainties.question: Which Features configuration is required for every General App?
uncertainties.impact: The number and type of configuration steps can expand substantially before Local Test.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official build flow is driven by the app's What, Where, and Which answers and presents Access, Surface, Embed, and Connect as conditional paths rather than one universal configuration.

uncertainties.question: Does Zoom Security Review add a wait before this local-test completion state?
uncertainties.impact: A required review could lengthen the path between scope selection and app addition.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart says that adding scopes submits a request and that unnecessary requests may be rejected, but it does not state whether or when that review blocks the documented Add App Now flow or provide a duration.

excluded_after_success: Executing a Zoom REST API request or validating a returned resource
Implementing or running a Video SDK, Meeting SDK, Zoom Apps SDK, Contact Center SDK, or Phone SDK feature
Receiving and verifying a webhook or WebSocket event
Generating and sharing an Authorization URL with internal users
Marketplace review, unlisted or public distribution, and publishing
